definition:
1. a block of stone  used as a prop  or buttress to a wall , (Iliad by Homer): a block  of rockcrystal, in which the Egyptian mummies were cased, (Herdotus Historicus) 
2. a block  or slab , bearing an inscription; and so, 
3. a gravestone , (Homer), attic 
4. a block  or slab , inscribed with record of victories, dedications, treaties, decrees, etc., (Herdotus Historicus), attic; γράφειν τινὰ εἰς στήλην, ἀναγράφειν ἐν στήλῃ, whether for honour, or for infamy, (Herdotus Historicus):—;also the record  itself, a contract, agreement , κατὰ τὴν στήλην according to agreement , (Aristophanes Comicus); στῆλαι αἱ πρὸς Θηβαίους (Demosthenes Orator) 
5. a boundary post, (Xenophon Historicus):—; the turning-post  at the end of the racecourse, Lat. meta, (Sophocles Tragicus) 
6. for Στῆλαι Ἡρακλήιαι, see. Ἡράκλειος. (ML)
